diagrams.title: ITS Architecture Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows how various components of Intelligent Transportation System connect - sensors/cameras on roads send data to central control room, which processes information and sends instructions to variable message signs, traffic signals, and provides information to drivers via GPS/apps.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how technology enables real-time traffic management and emission reduction

diagrams.title: Carbon Footprint Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of CO2 emissions per passenger-kilometer across different transport modes - showing car (highest), bus, train, and active transport (lowest).
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing which transport choices have lower environmental impact to encourage sustainable decisions

diagrams.title: Traffic Congestion Feedback Loop
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how congestion leads to increased emissions, which leads to more health issues, requiring more transport for healthcare access - a vicious cycle.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why reducing congestion has multiplier benefits for environment and health

diagrams.title: Green Travel Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Pyramid showing most to least sustainable travel options - from most preferred (telecommute) to least preferred (single-occupancy car), with public transport and cycling in between.
diagrams.purpose: Simple visual guide for making sustainable travel choices in order of environmental benefit
